title: Smart Lock Door System
description: Designed and implemented a prototype Smart Lock Door System to enhance residential security. As the Programmer for this academic group project, I wrote the microcontroller firmware to handle real-time access control using password authentication via a numeric keypad. The code efficiently processes user inputs and triggers the electronic locking mechanisms, providing a secure foundation for keyless smart home entry.

title: Water Level Control with Double Sensor
description: Developed an automated water level management system utilizing a dual-sensor configuration to prevent overflows. Serving as both the Hardware and Software Engineer, I integrated the primary and secondary fluid sensors with the central microcontroller and wrote the core logic. This redundancy ensures high system reliability; if the primary sensor fails, the secondary sensor acts as a fail-safe, instantly triggering the programmed output to regulate the water pumps.

title: Water Level Control using PID
description: Engineered an advanced, precision-driven water level control system. Acting as the Software Engineer, I programmed and tuned the Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller algorithm into the system. By implementing the control logic mathematically, the software dynamically adjusts the pump flow rates in real-time, drastically minimizing steady-state error and overshoot to maintain the exact targeted water level.

title: Smart Lettuce Monitor
description: Created an IoT-based environmental monitoring system tailored for hydroponic lettuce cultivation. As the Software Engineer and Web Designer, I developed the complete data pipeline from acquiring real-time sensor data (temperature, humidity, UV index) on the microcontroller to designing the user-friendly web interface. I built a responsive, live dashboard that visualizes this data, enabling users to monitor and optimize plant growth conditions seamlessly via the internet.

title: Smart Weight System Simulation
description: Conceptualized and directed a comprehensive virtual simulation for a Smart Weight System. Serving as the Concept Ideator and Team Lead, I designed the initial system architecture and guided the team in executing the simulation using microcontroller software. The project integrates an Arduino board, a matrix keypad, and an LCD module to process simulated load cell data against user-defined weight targets, serving as a robust proof-of-concept for future hardware deployment.
